articles.title: READ: Trump indictment related to hush money payment
Russian authorities detain suspect over St. Petersburg cafe blast
Hundreds of millions at risk from Chinese shopping app malware
Trump pleads not guilty to 34 felony counts
Ukraine strikes oil terminals near St. Petersburg in Russia
articles.summary: The long-range strikes are the latest salvo in Ukraine’s expanding campaign to inflict economic damage on Russia and hinder its war machine.

articles.title: US 250th Independence Day: Merz, Modi extend congratulations
articles.summary: The US is celebrating 250 years of independence with a massive fireworks display and military flyovers in the capital. Leaders from German Chancellor Friedrich Merz to India's PM Narendra Modi sent their well-wishes.

articles.title: Mali rebels target army bases, prison in fresh attacks
articles.summary: The army claimed it had regained control after the coordinated assaults on several towns and cities by Tuareg rebels and Islamists.

articles.title: Germany news: AfD elects leaders amid massive protests
articles.summary: Party delegates of the far-right AfD are meeting in Erfurt as tens of thousands of people are protesting the event. Meanwhile, Jürgen Klopp said he was "ready" to jump in as Germany's head coach. DW has more.

articles.title: Welcoming the Taliban? — Germany's new Afghanistan policy
articles.summary: To facilitate the deportation of criminals to Afghanistan, the German government is allowing Taliban diplomats into the country.

articles.title: Pope Leo calls on Europe to do more for migrants
articles.summary: The pontiff's trip to the migration frontline of Lampedusa underscored a stark message for Europe and the United States as intolerance and indifference rise.

articles.title: India news: Government lists 23 Pakistanis as 'terrorists'
articles.summary: Indian government has designated 23 Pakistan-based individuals as "terrorists" under its anti-terror law. Follow DW for more.

articles.title: Australia's Great Barrier Reef again avoids 'in danger' label
articles.summary: UNESCO acknowledged Australia's efforts towards the Great Barrier Reef's health but raised concerns over mass bleaching. For now, the popular marine site seems to have dodged the "endangered" tag.

articles.title: Trump set to put NATO unity to the test at Turkey summit
articles.summary: NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte is working to keep the alliance united and Donald Trump engaged. But disputes over burden sharing, US military commitments, and the Iran war are creating a major test for NATO unity.

articles.title: Kielce pogrom in Poland: 80th anniversary of 1946 massacre
articles.summary: Just over a year after World War II ended, a mob in the Polish city of Kielce killed around 40 Jews, some of them Holocaust survivors. A lie about ritual child murder escalated the violence.

articles.title: US: President Trump pardons 11 people, including Clean Air Act violators
articles.summary: Donald Trump has granted pardons to nine people convicted of violating a key environmental law. The US President has also pardoned an associate of convicted felon and former lobbyist Jack Abramoff.

articles.title: Iran: Khamenei's funeral ceremonies officially begin
articles.summary: Huge crowds have gathered in Tehran to pay their final respects to Iran's supreme leader, who was killed in an airstrike at the start of the US-Israeli campaign against Iran.

articles.title: Heat wave upends July Fourth celebrations across US
articles.summary: A dangerous heat wave has disrupted July Fourth events across the United States, including Donald Trump's flagship Great American State Fair, as the nation prepares to mark its 250th birthday.

articles.title: Taylor Swift, Travis Kelce tie knot in star-studded ceremony
articles.summary: The couple got married in a major star-studded celebration at Madison Square Garden over the Fourth of July weekend.

articles.title: NASA launches spacecraft to recover sinking telescope
articles.summary: The Swift telescope has been affected by recent solar storms and is sinking faster than ever, heading toward Earth. A NASA mission will try to capture it and boost its orbit.

articles.title: Lyra McKee: 3 men acquitted of killing N. Irish journalist
articles.summary: McKee was killed while covering clashes between Republicans and police in 2019. Judge Patricia Smyth said evidence was insufficient for a conviction, while McKee's family expressed disappointment.

articles.title: Maltese tycoon faces trial over Caruana Galizia murder
articles.summary: The Maltese businessman accused of masterminding journalist Daphne Caruana Galizia’s 2017 murder is in court — seven years after his arrest. Five other men have already been found guilty for their roles in the murder.

articles.title: Pope Leo defends US immigrant history at 250th anniversary
articles.summary: The first US pope said "successive waves of immigrants" played a role in shaping the United States, which marks its 250th anniversary on Saturday. Leo's remarks have made him a target of President Donald Trump.

articles.title: NATO summit: European members and Canada pledge long-term Ukraine aid
articles.summary: The NATO alliance's European members and Canada have signaled a readiness to fully take over the financing of Ukraine's fight against Russia.

articles.title: The moral test US founding father Thomas Jefferson failed?
articles.summary: Polish general and freedom fighter Thaddeus Kosciuszko presented US founding father Thomas Jefferson with a moral test. Historians still disagree on whether Jefferson passed or not.

articles.title: المغرب أول العابرين للربع بعد الانتصار على كندا، وصدام لاتيني أوروبي بين فرنسا وباراغواي
articles.summary: أسود الأطلس يحجزون بطاقة التأهل إلى ربع النهائي بعد الانتصار على كندا بثلاثية نظيفة، فيما تتجه الأنظار إلى قمة مثيرة تجمع فرنسا مع باراغواي

articles.title: فيديو.. عصام الحضري يهنئ منتخب المغرب بكلمات مؤثرة
articles.summary: أشاد حارس مرمى منتخب مصر السابق، عصام الحضري، بمسيرة المنتخب المغربي في كأس العالم 2026، مؤكدا أن أسود الأطلس أثبتوا مجددا أنهم من بين كبار المنتخبات العالمية بعد تأهلهم إلى الدور ربع النهائي.

articles.title: Disney Celebrates America live updates: Eagle-eye view of Great American State Fair
articles.summary: ABC is marking America's 250th anniversary with 24 hours of unparalleled programming called "Disney Celebrates America."

articles.title: Eala wins 'for all the girls with ruffled socks and chubby cheeks'
articles.summary: Alexandra Eala dedicates her stunning win over defending Wimbledon champion Iga Swiatek to "all the girls with ruffled socks and chubby cheeks".

articles.title: GB's Fery fights off Bergs and nosebleeds to win five-set epic
articles.summary: British wildcard Arthur Fery beats Zizou Bergs to reach the Wimbledon fourth round and keep home singles hopes alive - despite suffering three nosebleeds.

articles.title: The Funeral of Iran’s Supreme Leader, Ali Khamenei
articles.summary: Iran’s new leaders are commemorating Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, killed on the first day of the U.S.-Israeli war against the country, with a funeral unlike any other in recent history.

articles.title: Norfolk Island’s unique corals under triple threat from disease, El Niño and now government-approved dredging
articles.summary: Exclusive: Most of the island’s corals are likely to be species that have not been formally described by science, researcher says Get our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcast Scientists fear unique corals that fringe Australia’s remote Norfolk Island could disappear because of a tripl

articles.title: Doctors’ soaring use of AI scribes prompts Australian government warning over privacy
articles.summary: Exclusive: With the technology fast becoming popular in GP surgeries, regulators are monitoring its implementation and potential pitfalls Get our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcast The federal health department has raised concerns about the use of AI scribes by doctors as the health

articles.title: Germany’s Second Chance for Growth Rebound Starts Now
articles.summary: German data in the coming week showing the cumulative impact of the Iran war will set the stage for the government’s latest bid to awaken animal spirits in Europe’s biggest economy.

articles.title: خلافة المنكي.. انقسامات "داعش" تتيح معلومات استخباراتية
articles.summary: فاقمت الخسائر الأخيرة التي مني بها تنظيم داعش في نيجيريا من التوترات القائمة منذ فترة طويلة بين المقاتلين الأجانب في التنظيم والمجندين المحليين، مما أدى إلى انشقاقات واسعة مكنت السلطات الأمنية من الحصول على معلومات استخباراتية مهمة ساعدت في شن المزيد من الهجمات ضد معاقل التنظيم.

articles.title: مراسم تشييع شعبية لخامنئي في إيران بعد أكثر من أربعة أشهر على مقتله
articles.summary: مراسم التشييع الشعبية للمرشد الأعلى السابق آية الله علي خامنئي، انطلقت يوم السبت، بعد أكثر من أربعة أشهر على مقتله في اليوم الأول من الحرب الأمريكية الإسرائيلية، وتستمر مراسم الجنازة ستة أيام يزور خلالها الجثمان مدناً إيرانية ويقوم برحلة أخيرة خارج البلاد أيضاً.

articles.title: محمد بن زايد يهنئ ترامب بالذكرى الـ250 للاستقلال
articles.summary: هنأ الشيخ محمد بن زايد آل نهيان، رئيس دولة الإمارات، الرئيس الأميركي دونالد ترامب والشعب الأميركي بمناسبة الذكرى الـ250 لاستقلال الولايات المتحدة، مؤكدا حرص الإمارات على مواصلة تعزيز علاقات التحالف والصداقة بين البلدين.

articles.title: Morocco reach quarter-finals as co-hosts Canada knocked out
articles.summary: Canada become the first of co-hosts to exit the 2026 World Cup, after being beaten 3-0 by Morocco in Houston.

articles.title: GB's Fery battles to beat Bergs in five-set thriller
articles.summary: British wildcard Arthur Fery comes back from a set down twice to beat Zizou Bergs in a five set thriller, which lasted nearly five hours, as he reached the fourth round of Wimbledon, the first time he's done that in a Grand Slam.

articles.title: أوناحي يحقق رقما تاريخيا للعرب في كأس العالم
articles.summary: خطف نجم الوسط المغربي عز الدين أوناحي الأضواء من الجميع بالتألق في مواجهة كندا التي انتهت بفوز منتخب بلاده 3 / صفر، والتأهل لدور الثمانية ببطولة كأس العالم لكرة القدم 2026 التي تقام في الولايات المتحدة الأميركية وكندا والمكسيك.

articles.title: Jordan Bardella redit son "total soutien" à Marine Le Pen et "espère la voir élue"
articles.summary: Les deux leaders du parti d'extrême droite ont affiché leur unité alors qu'ils sauront mardi lequel sera candidat à l'Elysée.

articles.title: France-Paraguay : Koné remplace Tchouameni, aucune surprise dans la compo des Bleus... Suivez avec nous à 23 heures le huitième de finale de la Coupe du monde
articles.summary: L'équipe de France affronte le Paraguay, samedi à Philadelphie (Etats-Unis), à 23 heures, heure française.

articles.title: Morocco beats World Cup co-host Canada 3-0 and advances to the quarterfinals
articles.summary: Azzedine Ounahi scored twice to lead Morocco to a 3-0 win over Canada in the World Cup round of 16 Saturday to make the country the first African nation to reach the quarterfinals more than once

articles.title: منتخب المغرب يقهر "الاستثناء" ويؤكد "العالمية"
articles.summary: بثلاثية نظيفة، حسم المنتخب المغربي مواجهته أمام مضيفه الكندي في إطار دور الـ16 من نهائيات كأس العالم 2026، ليواصل مسيرة لافتة تؤكد أن ما حققه الفريق في مونديال قطر قبل أربع سنوات لم يكن مجرد ظرف استثنائي عابر، بل مشروعا كرويا متكاملا بدأ يفرض نفسه على خريطة الكرة العالمية.

articles.title: America 250: David Muir on national parks and Native tribes that call them home
articles.summary: As America marks its 250th birthday this Fourth of July, one of the best ways to celebrate is by honoring the natural wonders of the United States’ national parks.

articles.title: Record crowd hoping for home win - but will Antonelli 'disappear into distance'?
articles.summary: Close to 180,000 people are expected to attend Sunday's British Grand Prix but auguries for a home win are not that good, writes Andrew Benson.

articles.title: "روح قتالية نفخر بها".. محمد بن راشد يهنئ منتخب المغرب
articles.summary: هنأ الشيخ محمد بن راشد آل مكتوم، نائب رئيس دولة الإمارات، رئيس مجلس الوزراء، حاكم دبي، المنتخب المغربي بمناسبة تأهله إلى الدور ربع النهائي من نهائيات كأس العالم 2026، وذلك عقب الفوز الذي حققه أسود الأطلس اليوم على كندا بثلاثة أهداف دون رد.

articles.title: Scenes From Taylor Swift’s NYC Wedding: Fans, Stars and More
articles.summary: The scene outside Taylor Swift’s wedding in Manhattan had the drama and emotion of one of her albums.

articles.title: Venezuela Quake Death Toll Climbs to Nearly 3,000
articles.summary: Venezuela‘s official death toll from last week’s twin earthquakes rose to nearly 3,000 on Saturday.

articles.title: Warrington title hopes hit by dominant Leigh
articles.summary: Leigh Leopards cruise to victory against Warrington Wolves on day one of Magic Weekend at Everton's Hill Dickinson Stadium.

articles.title: NASA launched an emergency mission to stop the Swift Observatory from crashing to Earth
articles.summary: Engineers from Katalyst Space Technologies testing Link. | Image: NASA/Scott Wiessinger The Swift Observatory was launched in 2004, but recent solar storms have pushed its orbit lower, and it's in danger of burning up in Earth's atmosphere as soon as this year. To try and stave off its demise, NASA
